author: @Smo1k
text:
The funny thing about those two diagrams is that they both reverse the stator placement compared to how the textbook shows it. In the textbook, you move the magnet compared to the stator, not the other way around... But it's actually more efficient to have a static, coiled magnet and a free, magnetically open, operator.
 
😉

author: @Terminarch
text:
There's an idea I came across long ago. Damn near ALL energy on Earth is solar, although indirectly. Wind is from temperature differentials via sun. Plants, thus meat, thus muscle energy is sun. Plants, thus oil (and other burnables) is sun. Only things I can think of that's not solar is tides and nuclear...
Anyway, thoughts on the energy capture efficiency of being so far down the chain from source? Not that it matters in an emergency.

author: @mikejones-vd3fg
text: So cool, coincidently i had this thought while riding my bike the other day, didnt feel like my energy was being transfered efficiently or i was wasting lots of movement. It seemed i only put force on the top part of my stroke and the rest of the cycle the motion wasnt transfering any energy infact i was wasting energy to get back to the top.   I was trying to imagine a bike that let you use your arms to compensate, but then the video of the sewing machine comes out and explains everything why a bicycle isnt actually the most efficiecnt way.  I was imaginng a full body bicycle to compensate but the singer sewing machine figured out a better solution, although yours seems to be along tha path where you could use your arms to do the pushing and pulling and perhaps actually make a bicycle out of this design that lets you run on wheels!  But as a charger this looks way easier than hand cranking in a circle, well done.

replies.author: @eone2345
replies.text:
Circular motion such as in bicycle is good because it gives you momentum and dampening motion so you have the 'rest' time (for the lack of better word) for your muscle to extend and contract properly. If you have to pump lots of fluid using manual hand pump, you'll definitely want to use the one that has circular motion instead of linear one because of that. Linear one such as what people normally use in servicing is because they can be precisely controlled so they do not overfilled.

So you are not wasting any movement because bicycle is designed as simple transportation tool that people can pedal over a long distance using our muscle. You need a nice balance of force and rest period to be able to do that.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: hiya mate - I am sorry but that doesn't sound right - it just sounds like that's what you think - if what you are saying is right why do linear pumps predominate in hand pumping? why have the railways used pump trolleys? I think the only real reason some pumps are rotary is they are powered by motors so they have to be rotary - that's about it really

replies.author: @eone2345

replies.author: @William_Hada
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I just added a regulated 5v DC circuit to my Generator output and tried to charge my phone. Initially it started to charge the phone but soon cuts out. The amperage output is probably just not quite enough for a steady 5v charge. :(

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @William_Hada try charging a cap bank first mate

replies.author: @William_Hada
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkeringYes, that looks like the way to proceed. I tried some larger value capacitors and supercaps but it was taking a super long time to charge them to the 5V needed. I gradually dropped down to 2200 microfarads but I quess that is not enough capacity to sustain a stable charge on the phone. I'll continue to work on it. Thanks for the input!

author: @valveman12
text:
*First Comment🎇*
Nice design, Robert.  I made a similar generator but mine is foot powered, and charges a capacitor bank.  
The capacitor bank acts like a battery so you can stop running the generator, and let the charge in the capacitor bank continue to charge the phone, or whatever USB device you have.
